text.title: visibility-of-system-status Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for visibility-of-system-status as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Progress bars for indeterminate-length operations are misleading; they can only be accurate for operations with known total work units.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Showing a progress bar stuck at 50% for an unknown-duration operation frustrates users who cannot estimate completion time.

failure-mode.description: Over-notification: showing a success toast for every auto-save event creates notification fatigue and users stop noticing actual errors.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Auto-save confirmation should appear only after a pause in editing and dismiss after 2 seconds maximum; excessive notifications train users to ignore them.

failure-mode.description: Skeleton screens require designing the exact expected layout in advance; they break when the content structure differs from expectations.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Skeleton screens work well for predictable layouts like article lists but mislead users on complex dynamic content where actual layout differs from skeleton.

failure-mode.description: ARIA live regions for screen readers require careful implementation — aria-live="assertive" for errors, aria-live="polite" for non-critical updates; mixing them causes accessibility regressions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Screen reader users rely on live regions to hear status changes; incorrect politeness levels make updates either intrusive or invisible.

failure-mode.description: Offline/online status detection via navigator.onLine is unreliable; it detects network adapter state, not actual internet connectivity.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A user can appear online to the browser but unable to reach the internet; status indicators must actually test connectivity with real API calls.
